sprint: Sprint 35 -- V0.27.9 Bug Fixes (I-032: redo the I-031 fix that false-passed)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #8 in the V0.27 chain. V0.27.8 US-331 shipped synthetically-green but the real-world gate (deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 from Windows Git-Bash) reproduces the identical MSYS path-mangle from I-031. Branched from sprint/sprint34-bugfixes-V0.27.8 per chain-end-merge.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.8 baseline (V0.27.7 4204 + ~43 tests across US-331/333/334/335/336). Verify at sprint start. PRE-EXISTING failure: tests/lint/test_ralph_promise_tag_contract.py::test_promptMdAndRalphShDocumentSamePromiseTags (in-flight uncommitted offices/ralph/ralph.sh edit; not a regression). PRE-EXISTING ruff drift in tests/pi/regression/test_powersource_module_identity.py (8 findings, V0.24.1 file).

sprintNotes: GOAL: One story -- redo I-031's fix (US-331 false-passed) so deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 actually works from Windows Git-Bash.

WHY US-331 FALSE-PASSED: its tests/scripts/test_backfill_deploy_contexts.py exercised Python-level command-string construction but did NOT cover the actual shell-to-subprocess path-translation boundary that MSYS mangles. The regression test for US-337 MUST exercise that boundary.

CONSTRAINTS: (a) sprint_lint clean. (b) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM reproduced I-032 twice this session -- V0.27.8 deploy 15:04Z (Pi off) and 15:11Z (Pi reachable); both produced the byte-identical mangle error. (c) feedback_pm_validate_cli_in_cio_shell.md: US-337's real-world gate MUST be `bash deploy/deploy-server.sh` from the CIO's Windows Git-Bash, not just synthetic tests. (d) chain-end-merge: V0.27.9 stays on sprint branch.

POST-SPRINT-35 (NOT in sprint.json): (1) CIO re-runs deploy-server.sh from Windows Git-Bash post-V0.27.9 -> Step 4.6 completes; rows 11-15 populated. (2) V0.27 chain merge gate still: Drive 12 + Drain 18 + the four other V0.27.8 IRL gates.

stories.title: Fix US-331's MSYS path-mangle guard -- it doesn't trigger -- I-032
stories.intent: V0.27.8 US-331's MSYS_NO_PATHCONV-style guard for deploy-server.sh Step 4.6's remote sqlite3 invocation false-passed synthetic tests. The V0.27.8 deploy reproduced the byte-identical 'C:/Program Files/Git/home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db' mangle error. Fix the real shell-to-subprocess boundary; back it with a regression test that runs an actual subprocess (not just Python mocks).

filesToTouch: tests/scripts/test_backfill_deploy_contexts.py (UPDATE -- add a test that exercises a real subprocess.run with Git-Bash-like path translation; would FAIL pre-fix)
doNotTouch: scripts/apply_server_migrations.py loadServerCreds localhost-detection (V0.27.8 US-331 Context-2 fix; out of scope unless pre-flight finds it also broken)

groundingRefs.value: V0.27.8 deploy 2026-05-13 15:11Z (Pi reachable, V0.27.8 fix deployed): Step 4.6 produced byte-identical mangle 'Error: unable to open database C:/Program Files/Git/home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db'
Real-world gate: post-V0.27.9 bash deploy/deploy-server.sh from CIO's Windows Git-Bash -> Step 4.6 completes; server battery_health_log rows 11-15 populated matching Pi-side; 2nd deploy is no-op

acceptance: Pre-flight: verify the V0.27.8-deployed scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py on chi-srv-01 matches the repo (rule out I-032 hypothesis c); trace the exact construction of the mangled `C:/Program Files/Git/...` argument

Fix: under Git-Bash on Windows, the remote sqlite3 path is no longer MSYS-mangled (e.g., single-quote the remote command so only the Pi's shell expands $HOME, OR set MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 on the subprocess env, OR build the argv so the leading `/` is not Windows-drive-prefixable)
Regression test exercises a real subprocess.run boundary (not Python mocks alone) and FAILS pre-fix
invariants: Regression test FAILS pre-fix (real subprocess reproduces the mangle)
Real-world gate: bash deploy/deploy-server.sh from Windows Git-Bash populates rows 11-15; rerun no-op
Step 4.6 stays best-effort -- Pi-unreachable is a WARN, not a `set -e` blocker
stopConditions: If pre-flight finds the deployed script does NOT match the repo (I-032 hypothesis c) -- STOP; the bug is in the deploy pipeline, not the script
If the fix needs the shared subprocess runners in apply_server_migrations.py and that risks Step 4.5 -- STOP; scope the fix narrowly to the backfill path

grounding.value: Empirical RED-proof: with makeMsysSafePath() bypassed in scanPiRows (production line 560 swapped to passthrough), test_scanPiRows_pathSurvivesGitBashMsysMangleAtRealSubprocessBoundary raises BackfillError with the byte-identical 'C:/Program Files/Git/home/.../obd.db' error from the V0.27.8 deploy. With the fix restored, all 3 TestScanPiRowsSurvivesGitBashMsysMangle tests + the full 28-test test_backfill_deploy_contexts.py suite pass.
grounding.source: Rex (Ralph agent) 2026-05-13 -- run during US-337 crash-recovery iteration

grounding.value: Pre-flight (acceptance #1) -- hypothesis (c) ruled out: I-031 paper trail confirms `deploy-server.sh` Step 1 ran `git pull` and reported 'Already up to date' on the V0.27.8 deploy, so the deployed `scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py` matches the repo. The mangle construction trace: Git for Windows ssh.exe re-parses argv at the MSYS runtime layer, scanning for '/home/...' SUBSTRINGS within argv elements; the V0.27.8 env-only guard (MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 + MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL='*' on the subprocess env) is not honoured by the ssh.exe MSYS fork.
grounding.source: PM I-032 + I-031 issue files + V0.27.8 deploy output 2026-05-13 15:11Z

stories.completionNotes: US-337 / I-032 fix shipped at the argv FORM layer via new `makeMsysSafePath()` (doubles the leading '/' so MSYS's UNC-path heuristic '//host/share/...' leaves the token alone; POSIX collapses '//home/...' to '/home/...' on the remote so `sqlite3 -readonly` opens the same file). Applied at the wire boundary in `scanPiRows` ONLY (line 560) -- `PiCoordinates` stays tier-agnostic and the V0.27.8 env-only guard stays in place as belt-and-braces for tools that DO honour it. Regression coverage: real-subprocess `TestScanPiRowsSurvivesGitBashMsysMangle` class crosses an actual subprocess.run boundary against a Python shim simulating Git for Windows ssh.exe's MSYS argv re-parser. Pre-fix invariant verified empirically (this iteration is crash-recovery from a prior session that shipped the code but not the bookkeeping). All verification commands clean: pytest scripts+server suite green (1127 tests), ruff clean on scope files, bash -n clean on deploy-server.sh, sprint_lint 0 errors. Full suite has the documented PRE-EXISTING ralph.sh promise-tag failure called out in `testBaseline.note` (in-flight uncommitted edit; not a US-337 regression). Real-world gate (bash deploy/deploy-server.sh from CIO's Windows Git-Bash post-V0.27.9 deploy -> Step 4.6 completes; rows 11-15 populated) is the PM/CIO-side IRL validation, per the sprint's V0.27.9 chain-end-merge framing.

bigDefinitionOfDone: bash deploy/deploy-server.sh from the CIO's Windows Git-Bash shell post-V0.27.9 -> Step 4.6 completes; server battery_health_log rows 11-15 populated with end_timestamp/end_soc/runtime_seconds matching Pi-side; 2nd deploy is no-op (US-337 / I-032)
